rulings.Case Title: MONTGOMERY SANSOME LP VS. ZHIAN Z REZAI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 26, 2013, Line 2, PLAINTIFF MONTGOMERY SANSOME LP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion is denied as moot. Plaintiff filed this motion on March 20, 2013, and attacked the original answer filed on September 11, 2009. When defendant filed an amended answer on April 30, 2013, the amended answer rendered this motion moot. See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Sup Crt. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD J CONNER VS. COUSINS/MYERS II, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Third Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 26, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF EDWARD CONNER's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Third Amended Complaint. The motion to amend is granted in its entirety without any conditions. The motion substantially complies with CRC 3.1324. The encroachment and water discharge nuisance claims are distinct. The preferred practice is to have defendant test the merits of the claims by subsequent motion/demurrer. (See Cal. Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt., 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 280-281 (disapproved on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390). The amendments arise from the same transaction and are not prejudicial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MINDY FREILE VS. TIM LINCECUM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order 1) To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted; 2) Compelling Responses To Requests Fro Production Of Documents And 2) Compelling Responses To Form And Specially Prepared Interrogatories;
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, July 26, 2013, Line 1: Cross Complainant TIM LINCECUM'S Motion for an Order 1) to Have Requests for Admissions Deemed Admitted; 2) Compelling Responses to Requests for Production of Documents and 2) Compelling Responses to Form and Specially Prepared Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 501 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF'S PROCEDURAL OBJECTION IS DENIED. THE CORRECTION OF THE TIME OF THE HEARING BY ONE-HALF HOUR DID NOT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF AS IT DID NOT AFFECT THE DUE DATE OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION. FURTHER, THE MATTER WOULD NOT PROPERLY BE SET IN DEPARTMENT 501 AS IT INVOLVES DISCOVERY HANDLED BY THE PRO TEMPORE SYSTEM (SEE LOCAL RULE 8.10). PLAINTIFF OFFERED NO SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS TO GRANT DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER (1) TO HAVE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS DEEMED ADMITTED; (2) COMPELLING RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; (3) COMPELLING RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES; (4) COMPELLING RESPONSES TO SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES; AND (5) FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HER COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,625. RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED AND SANCTIONS SHALL BE PAID WITHIN TEN DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JUVENTINO ESTRADA VS. WELLS FARGO & COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 26, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT NATIONAL UNION FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY OF PITTSBURGH, PA, A PENNSYLVANIA COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To First Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Granted as to Plaintiff's responses to Interrogatory Nos. 3, 9, 15, 18, 21, 24, 27, 35, 38, 41, 44, and 47 (seeking identification of documents) and 1, 5, 6, 7, 16, 33, 36, 39, 42, 45, and 46 (seeking facts). Within 10 days, Plaintiff shall serve verified further responses. Denied as to Interrogatory Nos. 2, 8, 14, 17, 20, 23, 26, 34, 37, 40, and 43 (seeking identification of witnesses) and 10, 11, 13, 19, 22, 25, 28 and 29 (seeking facts). Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: MATT BLACK MR VS. HIGHTOWER PRODUCTION LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Production Of Rfp Demand No.1 Set 1 And For Monetary Sanction Against Hightower Production Llc And Irs Attorney Christina A Diedoardo Of The Law Offices Of Christina Diedoardo
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, July 26, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff MATT BLACK'S Motion for Order Compelling Production of RFP Demand No.1 Set 1 and for Monetary Sanction Against Hightower Production LLC And IRS Attorney Christina A Diedoardo of The Law Offices of Christina Diedoardo Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANT IN PART AND DENY IN PART. THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AS SET FORTH IN DEMAND NO. 1 OF SET NO. 1 IS GRANTED (EXCEPT THAT TAX RETURNS NEED NOT BE PRODUCED). SANCTIONS OF $3,290 ARE IMPOSED AGAINST DEFENDANT ONLY. THE MOTION IS DENIED TO THE EXTENT THAT IT SEEKS SANCTIONS OR ANY OTHER RELIEF AGAINST DEFENDANT'S FORMER COUNSEL CHRISTINA A. DIEDOARDO. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE SERVED AND SANCTIONS SHALL BE PAID BY DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN TEN DAYS OF SERVICE OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS BLASGEN VS. REFRACTIVE DIALER, LLC*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting Deft'S Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 26, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS BLASGEN'S Motion To Reconsider Court Order Granting Defendant's Petition To Compel Mediation And Arbitration. The Court clarifies its July 3, 2013, ruling and continues the matter to August 8, 2013. The Court treated Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration as a motion pursuant to CCP 473 and granted it. The Court intended to have a hearing on the merits on the petition to compel arbitration on July 26, 2013. Defendant was required to provide a courtesy copy of the petition to compel, Plaintiff was required to supply a courtesy copy of the opposition, and Defendant was given leave to file a substantive reply. Instead, Defendant did not provide a copy of the motion to compel arbitration (and any supporting papers) and plaintiff provided another copy of the motion for reconsideration. The Court has Plaintiff's opposition to the petition to compel arbitration only. The matter is continued to allow defendant to provide a courtesy copy of the petition to compel by July 31, 2013, and defendant has leave to file a substantive reply in support of the petition to compel by August 2, 2013. (The Court cannot access the original petition to compel and papers filed in support, and they are not scanned on the register of actions.) Please attach a cover letter to all courtesy copies reflecting the new hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ALLIANCE CREDIT SERVICES INC. VS. MARTIN ENG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, July 26, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG's Motion To Quash Service. The motion to quash service is denied. The process server's declaration establishes that four attempts were made by Plaintiff to personally serve Defendant at his residential address before substituted service was made. Plaintiff carried its burden of showing that reasonable attempts were made to personally serve Defendant before resorting to substituted service under CCP § 415.20(b). Defendant's declaration does not set forth sufficient evidence that establishes contrary facts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
